title: Glacier Sensor Net: A Drop-in Activation Across Stations
isolation: candidate runs as an isolated stdin->stdout subprocess via isorun; every station's data, labels, and the training/scoring code stay in the evaluator process
strategies: identity g(x)=x -> the network collapses to a linear classifier and reproduces the evaluator's linear baseline (~0.1 on every station)

ReLU / leaky-ReLU / swish / GELU -> unbounded rectifiers add nonlinearity and win big on the sharply nonlinear stations, but overfit the noisy near-linear sinusoidal station (below the linear baseline there) so the geometric mean punishes them
tanh / bounded saturating activations -> supply nonlinearity everywhere AND regularize via saturation, staying above the linear baseline on every station so the geometric mean stays high
a custom-shaped activation (piecewise samples on the grid) tuned for smooth saturation with the right gain / a small live negative branch, aiming to beat tanh on the remaining headroom
